phrases.intent_en: Can we sit on the terrace?
phrases.cultural_note: Terrace seating is popular in warm weather; service pace can be slower at peak times.

phrases.intent_en: Do you have a lunch menu?
phrases.cultural_note: Many brasseries offer a fixed-price lunch formula on weekdays.

phrases.intent_en: Could we have one glass of local champagne?
phrases.text_local: On pourrait avoir une coupe de champagne local ?
phrases.pronunciation: on poo-RAY ah-VWAR oon koop duh sham-PANY loh-KAL
phrases.cultural_note: Reims is a champagne city—"une coupe" usually means a glass served chilled.

phrases.intent_en: Do I need a reservation for tonight?
phrases.text_local: Il faut réserver pour ce soir ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel foh ray-zair-VAY poor suh swar
phrases.cultural_note: Evening spots near this square can fill up quickly on weekends.

responses.english: Yes, it is recommended on Saturday evening.
responses.phonetic: wee say pray-fay-RABL luh sam-DEE SWAHR
responses.french: Oui, c'est préférable le samedi soir.

phrases.intent_en: Could I have the bill, please?
phrases.cultural_note: In France, asking for the bill is normal—staff usually won't bring it automatically.
